title: Configure GitHub branch protection: require CI before merge on master
spec:
Configure branch protection rules on the `master` branch of the zazig-team/zazigv2 GitHub repo so that:

1. The `build-and-test` CI check (from `.github/workflows/ci.yml`) is a required status check before any PR can be merged.
2. Branches must be up to date with master before merging (strict status checks).
3. Admins are NOT exempt (enforce_admins: true).
4. Direct pushes to master are disallowed (all changes must go through a PR).

## Implementation

Use the `gh api` CLI (already authenticated on this machine). The check name to require is the GitHub Actions check name. Look it up first with:
```
gh api repos/zazig-team/zazigv2/branches/master/protection
```

Then check what the actual check run name is for the CI workflow:
```
gh api repos/zazig-team/zazigv2/commits/master/check-runs --jq '.check_runs[].name'
```

Then set branch protection:
```
gh api repos/zazig-team/zazigv2/branches/master/protection \
  --method PUT \
  --field 'required_status_checks={"strict":true,"contexts":["build-and-test"]}' \
  --field 'enforce_admins=true' \
  --field 'required_pull_request_reviews=null' \
  --field 'restrictions=null'
```

Adjust the context string if the actual check name differs from `build-and-test`.

Verify the protection was applied:
```
gh api repos/zazig-team/zazigv2/branches/master/protection
```

Write `.claude/branch-protection-report.md` with status: pass/fail and what was configured.
acceptance_tests:
Given the master branch of zazig-team/zazigv2 has no branch protection
When the agent configures branch protection via the GitHub API
Then `gh api repos/zazig-team/zazigv2/branches/master/protection` returns required_status_checks with the CI check name
And enforce_admins is true
And the branch requires a PR (allow_force_pushes is false)

Given a PR with failing CI exists
When someone attempts to merge it (including via gh pr merge)
Then GitHub rejects the merge with a status-check failure

Given a PR with passing CI exists
When someone merges it via gh pr merge --squash
Then the merge succeeds without error

title: Remove verify job type and all verify dead code from the pipeline
spec:
The `verify` job type is a legacy artifact. The orchestrator's `dispatchVerifyJobToMachine` function is dead code (never called). `handleVerifyResult` in agent-event still processes verify results that are never sent. This job removes all verify-related dead code and the `verify` job type from the pipeline.

## Files to change

### 1. New SQL migration (next sequential number after 240)
File: `supabase/migrations/241_remove_verify_job_type.sql`

Content: A migration that removes `verify` from the `jobs_job_type_check` constraint.
```sql
BEGIN;
ALTER TABLE public.jobs DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS jobs_job_type_check;
ALTER TABLE public.jobs ADD CONSTRAINT jobs_job_type_check CHECK (job_type IN (
  'code', 'infra', 'design', 'research', 'docs', 'bug',
  'persistent_agent', 'breakdown', 'combine', 'merge',
  'deploy_to_test', 'deploy_to_prod', 'review', 'feature_test', 'ci_check', 'test'
));
COMMIT;
```
(Do NOT remove `verify_context` column or `verify_failed` status — these may exist in historical records.)

### 2. `supabase/functions/orchestrator/index.ts`
- Remove the `dispatchVerifyJobToMachine` function entirely (lines around 1076-1100)
- Remove imports of `VerifyJob`, `VerifyResult` from shared messages if they are only used by this dead function

### 3. `supabase/functions/agent-event/handlers.ts`
- Remove the `handleVerifyResult` export function (around line 498)
- Remove imports of `VerifyResult`, `VerifyJob` if unused after removal

### 4. `supabase/functions/agent-event/index.ts`
- Remove `"verify_result"` from the handled event types list (around line 59)
- Remove the `case "verify_result":` dispatch to `handleVerifyResult`

### 5. `packages/local-agent/src/index.ts`
- In the `case "verify_job":` handler (around line 198), replace the verify call with a deprecation log:
  ```ts
  case "verify_job":
    console.warn(`[local-agent] Received deprecated verify_job message — ignoring (verify step removed)`);
    break;
  ```
  Do NOT remove the case entirely — the OrchestratorMessage union still includes VerifyJob for backward compat.

### 6. `supabase/functions/_shared/pipeline-utils.ts`
- Remove `"verify"` from the `NON_IMPLEMENTATION_TYPES` Set (around line 549)

### 7. Update tests
- Check `packages/shared/src/messages.test.ts` for any verify-related tests that reference this behavior — leave type tests in place (VerifyJob/VerifyResult types stay in the shared protocol for backward compat), but remove any tests that assert on the now-removed `handleVerifyResult` behavior.
- Check `packages/local-agent/src/verifier.test.ts` — the verifier module itself can stay (it's tested code), but remove any integration tests that assert the orchestrator dispatches verify jobs.

## What NOT to change
- Do NOT remove `VerifyJob`, `VerifyResult` types from `packages/shared/src/messages.ts` — they are part of the wire protocol and removing them would be a breaking change for any agents still in flight.
- Do NOT remove `verify_context` column or `verify_failed` job status — historical records use these.
- Do NOT touch `packages/local-agent/src/verifier.ts` — it may be used in tests and can stay as a library module even if not dispatched.
acceptance_tests:
Given the codebase after this job is applied
When `grep -r 'handleVerifyResult' supabase/functions/` is run
Then no results are returned

Given the codebase after this job is applied
When `grep -r 'dispatchVerifyJobToMachine' supabase/functions/` is run
Then no results are returned

Given the migration 241_remove_verify_job_type.sql is applied
When attempting to insert a job with job_type = 'verify'
Then the database rejects it with a check constraint violation

Given the local agent receives a legacy verify_job message
When the message handler processes it
Then it logs a deprecation warning and does not call verifier.verify()

Given the codebase builds and tests pass
When `npm run build && npm run test` is run from the repo root
Then all checks pass with no TypeScript errors

title: Add pre-merge CI gate to job-merger role prompt
spec:
The current `job-merger` role prompt merges a PR as long as `mergeable: true` without verifying that CI checks have actually passed. If branch protection (from the companion job) blocks the merge, the agent will waste a retry cycle. This job updates the job-merger prompt to explicitly check CI status before attempting to merge.

## Implementation

Create a new SQL migration: `supabase/migrations/242_job_merger_ci_gate.sql`

The migration UPDATEs the `job-merger` role prompt. The key change is step 1:

Replace the current step 1:
```
1. Check PR mergeability first: `gh pr view <featureBranch> --json mergeable,mergeStateStatus`
2. If mergeable: merge immediately with `gh pr merge <featureBranch> --squash --delete-branch` and finish
3. If conflicting: [rebase flow]
```

With a new step 1 that checks CI:
```
1. Check CI status and mergeability:
   `gh pr view <prUrl> --json mergeable,mergeStateStatus,statusCheckRollup`
   - If mergeStateStatus == "blocked": report FAILED — PR is blocked by branch protection or required reviews
   - If mergeStateStatus == "dirty": report FAILED — PR has merge conflicts
   - If mergeStateStatus == "behind": rebase flow (step 3 below)
   - If mergeStateStatus == "unstable" or any statusCheckRollup state is "FAILURE" or "PENDING": report FAILED — CI has not passed
   - If mergeStateStatus == "clean": proceed to merge in step 2
2. Merge: `gh pr merge <prUrl> --squash --delete-branch`
3. Conflict/rebase flow: [existing rebase logic]
```

Write the full updated prompt in the migration. Reference the existing prompt in migration 155 (`supabase/migrations/155_merge_reliability_prompt_updates.sql`) as the base — update it with the CI gate step added.

Use `UPDATE public.roles SET prompt = $$ ... $$ WHERE name = 'job-merger';` (unconditional update, no WHERE guard on prompt content).
acceptance_tests:
Given a feature PR where CI checks are still pending
When the job-merger agent processes the merge job
Then it reads the PR mergeStateStatus and finds it is 'unstable'
And it writes status: fail to job-merger-report.md with a message that CI has not passed
And it does NOT call gh pr merge

Given a feature PR where CI has passed (mergeStateStatus == 'clean')
When the job-merger agent processes the merge job
Then it calls gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch
And writes status: pass to job-merger-report.md

Given the migration 242_job_merger_ci_gate.sql is applied
When `SELECT prompt FROM roles WHERE name = 'job-merger'` is run
Then the result contains the text 'mergeStateStatus' and 'unstable'
And the result contains the text 'statusCheckRollup'

title: Escalate failed test jobs: notify CPO and fail feature after exhaustion
acceptance_tests:
Given a test job for a feature has just failed
When handleJobFailed processes it
Then the CPO receives a notification that the test job failed and human review is required
And the feature remains in writing_tests status (not auto-failed immediately)

Given a test job for a feature has been in failed state for more than 24 hours
When processFeatureLifecycle runs its writing_tests check
Then the feature status is updated to 'failed'
And the CPO receives a notification that the feature auto-failed due to test job failure

Given a feature with no spec and no acceptance_tests
When triggerTestWriting is called after breakdown
Then the feature transitions to building directly with no test job created
And the CPO is notified that test writing was skipped

Given a feature with a spec
When the test job for that feature fails after 12 hours
Then processFeatureLifecycle does NOT auto-fail the feature (less than 24h threshold)
And the feature remains in writing_tests

Given the codebase builds and tests pass
When npm run build and npm run test are run from the repo root
Then all checks pass
